evals.prompt: Write a Rust function that reads a config file at ~/.config/myapp/config.toml, parses it into a Config struct, and returns an error if the file is missing or malformed. The config has fields: port (u16), host (String), and log_level (String). Use proper error handling — don't use unwrap anywhere.
evals.expected_output: A Rust function using Result and the ? operator, serde-compatible TOML parsing, useful path context, and error types that distinguish I/O from parse failures without requiring an unnecessary dependency.
Idiomatic rewrite using iterators, entry API, removing unnecessary clones by taking ownership or using references. No unwrap calls. Using &[Person] instead of &Vec<Person> in the parameter.

evals.prompt: I'm building a library crate for a TCP connection manager. The connection can be in three states: Disconnected, Connecting, and Connected. Only Connected connections can send data. Only Disconnected connections can start connecting. I want to make invalid states unrepresentable at compile time. How should I design this? Write the full implementation.
evals.expected_output: Type state pattern implementation using PhantomData, with proper state transitions (Disconnected::connect -> Connecting, Connecting::on_established -> Connected), and send() only available on Connection<Connected>. Each state transition consumes self and returns the new state.
